creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
/sbin/savecore checks the freespace on the dump directory before saving a core, but it's file_size estimate is so wildly conservative that it's basically useless IMHO. The estimate does not consider the effects of sparseness (~ 3.5x space reduction), ZFS compression (~ 32x space reduction), or gzip compression (~ 68x space reduction).

I disabled free space checks and submitted differential revision D2587.  However, I then learned that Isilon is planning to merge support for compressing core files directly on the dump device.  Once that change is in place, savecore's free space check will once again be useful.  The new behavior should be like this:

If core dump is compressed
   If /var/crash has sufficient space
       Write compressed core file
else
    if user specified "-z"
        Try to write compressed core file
    else
        Try to write sparse core file
If the write succeeded
    Clear the core from the dump device
else
    Delete the partial core file
    Log an error message

This task is blocked by D2587 and D3057.  Neither review has an accompanying PR.
